title: Approved living-world sequence: day-part, weather, naturalist prompts, field partner
summary: Fourteenth structured packet for Eco Explorer. The user has explicitly approved the living-world direction, and the first four slices are now aligned. Day-part and weather are live and reviewed cleanly, the notebook-style naturalist prompt layer is live, and the compact journal-surface follow-up now reviews cleanly as well. The first field-partner pass also landed without destabilizing the UI, but `critic-22` found that it still collapses the scout handoff into four family-generic lines and has not yet adopted the fuller cue-bank cadence plan. The next active living-world step is therefore a larger authored follow-up pass, after which a clean review can hand the lane forward to the parked phenology sequence.

execution_order.goal: Add a simple day-part system through a shared deterministic world-state helper, starting with `dawn`, `day`, and `dusk`.
execution_order.why_now: Time-of-day is the lowest-risk first living-world slice, and the day-part mood matrix is now specific enough to support implementation without reopening broad feature design.

execution_order.goal: Review the first day-part pass for readability, atmosphere, and world-state scope discipline.
execution_order.why_now: The first living-world slice needs a dedicated critique pass before weather builds on top of it.

execution_order.goal: Align the shared day-part cycle to a forward-feeling temporal order without losing the calm visual-first scope of the first pass.
execution_order.why_now: Weather, notebook prompts, and the field partner will all inherit awkward timing language if the core seam stays `day -> dusk -> dawn`.

execution_order.goal: Review the day-part ordering follow-up for temporal clarity, readability, and scope discipline.
execution_order.why_now: The first world-state seam is worth rechecking once its temporal order is cleaned up, because weather should build on the corrected version, not the awkward one.

execution_order.goal: Add biome-specific weather ambience by extending the same shared world-state helper with one calm weather profile per biome family.
execution_order.why_now: Weather builds on the day-part feel and can stay mostly visual and atmospheric at first if it does not invent a separate simulation.

execution_order.goal: Review the first weather pass for readability, biome distinction, and world-state scope discipline.
execution_order.why_now: Weather is the next living-world layer and should get a dedicated critique pass before notebook prompts build on it.

execution_order.goal: Add notebook-style observation prompts using biome, zone, world-state, and ecosystem-note context signals.
execution_order.why_now: The weather pass is now verified clean, so the next strongest living-world gain is one calm observation lens that can use place, time, weather, and journal context together.

execution_order.goal: Refit the notebook-prompt journal surface so prompts stay readable and additive instead of replacing unlocked ecosystem-note teaching.
execution_order.why_now: The first prompt pass is worth keeping, but the surface needs one compact readability/additivity follow-up before the field partner builds on top of it.

execution_order.goal: Prepare a compact authored field-partner cue bank and silence-state matrix that can support the first implementation without pushing it toward chatter.
execution_order.why_now: This can run in parallel with `main-34` and reduce the risk that the first field-partner pass improvises tone, cadence, or suppression rules.

execution_order.goal: Add a lightweight field partner layer built on the same deterministic world-state and naturalist context signals.
execution_order.why_now: The companion will be stronger if it speaks about real living-world signals instead of being added as a generic overlay first, but it should wait until the notebook-prompt surface is stable.

execution_order.goal: Review the first field-partner pass for tone, silence discipline, science grounding, and HUD restraint.
execution_order.why_now: The first companion pass needed a dedicated critique before the living-world lane moved on, because even a calm-feeling partner can still drift into generic or under-authored territory.

execution_order.goal: Replace the family-generic field-partner lines with a small authored cue bank plus the fuller silence and cadence plan from the scout handoff.
execution_order.why_now: The first pass is safe enough to keep, but the next real quality gain is making it biome-local and cue-aware before phenology or later partner growth builds on a too-generic seam.

execution_order.goal: Review the authored field-partner follow-up and promote phenology if the companion layer is now stable enough to carry forward.
execution_order.why_now: A clean authored follow-up should unblock the next larger living-world chunk instead of leaving the lane stalled on tiny partner corrections.

guardrails: Science accuracy remains a hard gate for every prompt, cue, and environmental change.
These systems should deepen noticing and atmosphere, not add punishment or urgency.
Keep HUD growth minimal and playfield-safe.
Build the sequence on one shared deterministic world-state helper instead of four disconnected feature systems.
Day-part should start with `dawn`, `day`, and `dusk`; any moonlit variant is a later readability-gated stretch, not a v1 requirement.
Weather should start with `clear` plus one calm profile per biome family and stay render-first before any broader emphasis or spawn effects.
Chronological day-part labels should move in a forward-feeling order before weather or prompt language builds on them.
Naturalist prompts and the field partner should remain optional, warm, and non-quiz-like.
Naturalist prompts should stay relationship-first: shelter, timing, neighbor-role, and comparison cues grounded in local evidence.
The first notebook-prompt pass should start from a small authored seed set with simple evidence-aware unlock and rotation rules instead of broad runtime chatter.
Notebook prompts should not displace unlocked ecosystem-note teaching; the compact journal surface needs room for both the stable relationship and the current observation lens.
Prompt and partner surfaces should stay distinct: prompts live in the compact notebook flow first, while the partner uses a tiny transient in-biome strip only when needed.
Field-partner delivery should feel like a notebook margin and default to silence during overlays, map travel, active inspect, transitions, and repeated same-state revisits.
The first field-partner pass should use a small authored cue bank plus explicit silence, cooldown, and repeat-suppression rules instead of freeform runtime chatter.
Field-partner follow-up work should replace family-only paraphrases with cue-specific authored lines before the feature expands any further.
Field-partner cadence should include both cue-level dedupe and a meaningful quiet window, not just a per-visit line cap.
Direct API mode is not required for the first naturalist or field-partner implementation.
The day-part and weather foundations are now stable enough for notebook prompts, but keep `main-34` ordered behind the prompt-surface follow-up rather than overlapping them.
acceptance_focus: Living-world features land in a calm, ordered sequence.
The first implementation slices stay atmospheric and readable.
The sequence grows from shared world-state signals instead of one-off feature logic.
The companion/naturalist layers are built on real world-state signals rather than floating above the game.
handoff_prompt: Read AGENTS.md, .agents/project-memory.md, .agents/work-queue.md, docs/reports/2026-03-28-living-world-sequence.md, docs/reports/2026-03-28-living-world-grounding-handoff.md, docs/reports/2026-03-28-day-part-mood-matrix-handoff.md, docs/reports/2026-03-28-first-day-part-review.md, docs/reports/2026-03-28-day-part-ordering-follow-up-review.md, docs/reports/2026-03-28-weather-readability-matrix-handoff.md, docs/reports/2026-03-29-weather-pass-review.md, docs/reports/2026-03-28-notebook-prompt-handoff.md, docs/reports/2026-03-28-weather-aware-notebook-prompts-handoff.md, docs/reports/2026-03-28-field-partner-tone-boundaries-handoff.md, docs/reports/2026-03-28-prompt-partner-surface-handoff.md, docs/reports/2026-03-29-notebook-prompt-review.md, docs/reports/2026-03-29-notebook-prompt-follow-up-review.md, docs/reports/2026-03-29-field-partner-cue-bank-handoff.md, docs/reports/2026-03-29-field-partner-review.md, and this packet. The first field-partner pass is live and stable enough to keep, but the next active step is `main-50`: replace the family-only partner copy with the authored cue bank, adopt the fuller silence and cooldown plan, and keep the strip tiny so a clean follow-up review can hand the lane onward to phenology.
